rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verified Proper Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two, And Set Two Form Interrogatory No. 17.1
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion to compel verified proper responses to requests for admission, set two, and set two form interrogatory no. 17.1 is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that it properly met and conferred prior to filing this motion. (C.C.P. 2033.290(b), 2030.300(b), 2016.040.) Defendant violated the order made by this Court during the April 2, 2014 CMC that meet and confer needed to be conducted by actually talking to one another. Even ignoring that violation, defendant's alleged attempt to meet and confer did not demonstrate any willingness or intent to resolve the issue informally. Finally, this Court's order granting preference required that the parties have an informal discovery meeting with the Court prior to any discovery motion hearing, and defendant failed to have such meeting. Even if the motion was considered on its merits, it would be denied. Plaintiffs' responses to requests for admission nos. 32, 33, 37 and 38 are sufficient. Plaintiffs' amended responses to form interrogatories moot defendant's motion as to that issue. A hearing is required regarding whether sanctions will be imposed. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Vwgoas Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Preclude Depositions Of Richard Bandstra, Thomas Zorn, And Further Deposition Of Defendant Volkswagen Group Of America On Irrelevant Topics
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion for protective order to preclude depositions of Richard Bandstra and Thomas Zorn and a further deposition of defendant: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
